statute: Rhode Island General Laws § 5-65 (Contractors' Registration and Licensing Board)
license_notes: RI requires all contractors to register with the Contractors Registration and Licensing Board (CRLB). Includes 5-hour pre-education courses. GL insurance required ($500,000 min — effective 2024). Unregistered contractors have no lien rights.

mandatory_clauses.label: RI CRLB registration disclosure
Insurance disclosure (required)
Mechanics lien notice
mandatory_clauses.text:
NOTICE OF LIEN RIGHTS

Under state law, contractors, subcontractors, and material suppliers who provide labor or materials for your property and are not paid may have the right to file a mechanic's lien against your property. Even if you pay your general contractor in full, you may still be subject to liens from unpaid subcontractors and suppliers. Protect yourself by obtaining written lien waivers from all parties before making final payment.

mandatory_clauses.label: Federal 3-day right to cancel (TILA)
Not legal advice disclaimer
mandatory_clauses.text: DISCLAIMER: This contract was generated by ContractorDesk as a starting point template. It does not constitute legal advice. Contractors are encouraged to have a licensed attorney review agreements for their specific situation and applicable state requirements.

penalties_for_non_compliance: 1st violation up to $5,000; subsequent up to $10,000 each. Unregistered contractors have no lien rights.
notes: Rhode Island requires registration with CRLB for all contractors. Includes 5-hour pre-registration education. GL insurance required: $500,000 minimum (new requirement effective 2024) with CRLB listed as certificate holder. Commercial roofing, well-drillers, and home inspectors require actual licenses (not just registration). Electrical requires DWS license. Unregistered contractors have no mechanics lien rights.
